description: CLI interface for Raptive traffic and revenue data

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

options.name: --force
options.help: Clear existing credentials and re-authenticate

options.name: --credential-type
options.help: Authenticate only this credential type (e.g., 'oauth', 'browser_session')

login.usage_instructions: Use to authenticate with Raptive via browser. Use --profile/-p for a named profile. Use --force/-F to re-authenticate.

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Use to clear stored credentials. Use --profile/-p for a specific profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

status.usage_instructions: Use to check current authentication status.

refresh.help: Refresh OAuth access token using stored refresh token.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

refresh.usage_instructions: Use to refresh an expired token.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

options.name: --verbose
options.help: Show detailed checks

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name (defaults to all profiles)

test.usage_instructions: Use to verify credentials are valid. Use --verbose/-v for detailed output.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --filter
options.help: Filter: field:op:value (e.g., is_default:eq:True)

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use to list all profiles and see which is the default.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

get.usage_instructions: Use to get details of a specific profile. NAME is required.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Use to create a new profile. NAME is required.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Use to switch the active profile. NAME is required.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.

options.name: --force
options.help: Skip confirmation

delete.usage_instructions: Use to delete a profile. NAME is required. Use --force/-F to skip confirmation.

profiles.usage_instructions: Profile management -- list, get, create, set default, and delete authentication profiles.

auth.usage_instructions: Authentication management -- login via browser, logout, check status, refresh tokens, test credentials.

summary.help: Get dashboard summary metrics for a date range. Shows earnings, RPM, sessions, and pageviews.

options.name: --period
options.help: Time period: yesterday, last7d, last30d, mtd, lastmonth

options.name: --start
options.help: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD). Overrides --period

options.name: --end
options.help: End date (YYYY-MM-DD). Overrides --period

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

summary.usage_instructions: Use to get high-level metrics (earnings, RPM, sessions, pageviews) for a date range. Default period is last30d. Use --period or --start/--end for custom dates.

date-bounds.help: Get the date bounds for available data. Shows the earliest and latest dates with data.

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

date-bounds.usage_instructions: Use to check the earliest and latest dates with available data.

dashboard.usage_instructions: Dashboard metrics -- get summary metrics (earnings, RPM, sessions, pageviews) and check data date bounds.

overview.help: Get daily earnings breakdown for a date range. Shows earnings, sessions, RPM for each day.

options.name: --period
options.help: Time period: yesterday, last7d, last30d, mtd, lastmonth

options.name: --start
options.help: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD). Overrides --period

options.name: --end
options.help: End date (YYYY-MM-DD). Overrides --period

options.name: --filter
options.help: Filter results (field:op:value). Operators: eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, in, like, contains

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of results to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

overview.usage_instructions: Use to get daily earnings breakdown. Default period is last30d. Use --period or --start/--end for custom dates. Use --filter/-f for field-based filtering (e.g., 'earnings:gt:100').

by-device.help: Get earnings breakdown by device type. Shows earnings for Desktop, Mobile, and Tablet.

options.name: --filter
options.help: Filter results (field:op:value). Operators: eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, in, like, contains

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of results to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

by-device.usage_instructions: Use to see earnings split by Desktop, Mobile, and Tablet. Use --filter/-f to filter by device or earnings amount.

by-page.help: Get earnings breakdown by page. Shows page-level performance metrics sorted by pageviews.

options.name: --period
options.help: Time period: yesterday, last7d, last30d, mtd, lastmonth

options.name: --start
options.help: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD). Overrides --period

options.name: --end
options.help: End date (YYYY-MM-DD). Overrides --period

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of pages to return

options.name: --min-pageviews
options.help: Minimum pageviews filter (server-side)

options.name: --max-pageviews
options.help: Maximum pageviews filter (server-side)

options.name: --min-rpm
options.help: Minimum page RPM filter (server-side)

options.name: --max-rpm
options.help: Maximum page RPM filter (server-side)

options.name: --search
options.help: Search term to filter page URLs (server-side, contains match)

options.name: --filter
options.help: Filter results (field:op:value). Operators: eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, in, like, contains

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

by-page.usage_instructions: Use to see per-page earnings. Default limit is 20. Use --min-pageviews, --max-pageviews, --min-rpm, --max-rpm for server-side filtering. Use --search/-q to filter by URL.

by-traffic-source.help: Get earnings breakdown by traffic source. Shows performance metrics for each traffic source (direct, google, etc.).

options.help: Time period: yesterday, last7d, last30d, mtd, lastmonth

options.name: --start
options.help: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD). Overrides --period

options.name: --end

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of results to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

by-traffic-source.usage_instructions: Use to see earnings by traffic source (direct, google, etc.). Supports --period or --start/--end date range.

brand-safety.help: Get brand safety assessments for pages. Shows pages with brand safety ratings sorted by pageviews.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of pages to return

options.name: --filter
options.help: Filter results (field:op:value). Operators: eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, in, like, contains

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

brand-safety.usage_instructions: Use to see brand safety assessments for pages. Default limit is 20. Shows safety ratings sorted by pageviews.

sources.help: Get earnings breakdown by ad network source. Shows monthly earnings from each ad network.

options.name: --filter
options.help: Filter results (field:op:value). Operators: eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, in, like, contains

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of results to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

sources.usage_instructions: Use to see earnings by ad network source (e.g., AdSense). Shows monthly earnings from each network.

earnings.usage_instructions: Revenue analytics -- daily earnings overview, earnings by device, page, traffic source, country, category, ad network source, and brand safety assessments.

sources.help: Get traffic breakdown by source. Shows sessions from different traffic sources (direct, google, bing, etc.). Note: The Raptive API does not support date filtering for this endpoint. Data reflects the default dashboard period.

options.name: --filter
options.help: Filter results (field:op:value). Operators: eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, in, like, contains

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of results to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

sources.usage_instructions: Use to see session counts by traffic source. Note: Raptive API does not support date filtering for this endpoint.

by-device.help: Get traffic breakdown by device type. Shows sessions for Desktop, Mobile, and Tablet.

options.name: --filter
options.help: Filter results (field:op:value). Operators: eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, in, like, contains

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of results to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of properties to include

options.name: --table
options.help: Display as table

by-device.usage_instructions: Use to see sessions split by Desktop, Mobile, and Tablet.

traffic.usage_instructions: Traffic analytics -- session data by traffic source and device type.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Use to clear all cached API responses and free disk space.

cache.usage_instructions: Cache management -- clear cached API responses.

usage_instructions: Use for viewing Raptive (ad network) traffic and revenue data. Commands follow `raptive <group> <action>` pattern. Most commands support --period (yesterday, last7d, last30d, mtd, lastmonth) or custom --start/--end dates. Supports --table/-t, --filter/-f, --properties/-p, --limit/-l. Use --no-cache to bypass response cache. Requires browser authentication via `raptive auth login`.
